message:
update(parser,demo,beads,evidence): DOT edge groups, port stripping, latest-edit-wins render pipeline

DOT parser improvements (crates/fm-parser/src/dot_parser.rs):
- Add edge group expansion: "A -> {B C D}" now correctly fans out
  into three separate edges (A->B, A->C, A->D)
- Strip DOT port/compass suffixes from node IDs: "node:port:n"
  normalizes to "node", preventing duplicate node creation
- Inline format args in warning messages for cleaner code
- Add three new unit tests covering port stripping, edge group
  expansion, and compass point handling

Demo showcase (frankenmermaid_demo_showcase.html):
- Implement latest-edit-wins render pipeline with revision tracking,
  debounced scheduling, and automatic cancellation of stale runs
- Add render button busy state with spinner and queued/rendering labels
- Show per-stage timing, revision number, and error state in the
  parse summary and render chips
- Replace inline render calls with scheduleRender() for consistent
  debounce and staleness discard behavior
- Wire up input event listener for live-as-you-type rendering

Evidence (evidence/demo_strategy.md):
- Add design rule: live authoring loop must be latest-edit-wins with
  stale async discard and visible stage timing/error state

Beads (.beads/issues.jsonl):
- Update bead status and metadata to reflect parser and demo progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2nw.10","title":"Complete all remaining diagram type parsers (12 types)","description":"# Complete All Remaining Diagram Type Parsers

Implement parsers for all remaining Mermaid diagram types not yet supported to achieve 100% type coverage.

## Current Status

Implemented (12 types):
- flowchart ✓
- sequence ✓
- class ✓
- state ✓
- er ✓
- gitgraph ✓
- gantt ✓
- pie ✓
- mindmap ✓
- timeline ✓
- journey ✓
- quadrant ✓

## Remaining (12 types)

### Priority 1 (Common)
1. **sankey** - Flow diagrams with width-proportional edges
2. **c4context** - C4 architecture context diagrams
3. **c4container** - C4 container diagrams
4. **c4component** - C4 component diagrams

### Priority 2 (Moderate)
5. **c4dynamic** - C4 dynamic diagrams
6. **c4deployment** - C4 deployment diagrams
7. **architecture** - Architecture diagrams
8. **block** - Block diagrams

### Priority 3 (Specialized)
9. **kanban** - Kanban boards
10. **xychart** - XY charts
11. **radar** - Radar/spider charts
12. **treemap** - Treemap visualizations

## Implementation Pattern

For each diagram type:

```rust
fn parse_<type>(input: &str, builder: &mut IrBuilder) {
    // 1. Skip header line
    // 2. Parse type-specific directives
    // 3. Parse nodes/elements
    // 4. Parse relationships/edges
    // 5. Add warnings for unsupported syntax
}
```

## Per-Type Requirements

### Sankey
- Source → Target with value
- Auto-calculated widths
- Color groups

### C4 Types
- Person, System, Container, Component elements
- Relationship with description
- Boundary grouping
- External vs internal marking

### Architecture
- Groups and services
- Junctions and edges
- Icons and labels

### Block
- Columns and blocks
- Arrows between blocks
- Spacing directives

### Kanban
- Lanes (todo, doing, done)
- Cards with metadata
- WIP limits

### XY Chart
- Axes with ranges
- Line, bar, area series
- Data points

### Radar
- Axes (spokes)
- Data series
- Fill areas

### Treemap
- Hierarchical data
- Size values
- Color mapping

## Testing Requirements

Each type needs:
- 5+ positive test cases
- 3+ error recovery cases
- 1+ large input stress test
- Golden output comparison

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 24 diagram types parseable
- Detection working for all types
- Error recovery for malformed input
- Test coverage for each type
- Documentation of syntax supported","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:17:54.309853176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:04:41.272778039Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:04:41.272044629Z","close_reason":"All 24 diagram type parsers are implemented in fm-parser/src/mermaid_parser.rs. Each has dedicated parse functions with error recovery. Detection covers exact, fuzzy, content heuristic, and DOT format strategies.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagram-types","parser"],"comments":[{"id":74,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":103,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.10","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Per-Diagram-Type Tests
For each of 12 remaining types:
1. Basic syntax parsing
2. All statement types supported
3. Direction/orientation handling
4. Style/class application
5. Error recovery edge cases

### Diagram Types to Implement
- Quadrant chart, ZenUML, Timeline, Mindmap
- Sankey, User Journey, C4, Architecture
- Kanban, Block, Radar/XY, Treemap/Requirement/Packet

### Corpus Tests
Use mermaid-js test corpus for compatibility:
1. Parse all mermaid-js examples
2. Compare IR structure with expected
3. Track parser coverage metrics

### Regression Tests
Golden output for each diagram type sample

### Logging Requirements
- Parse trace with token/AST details
- Unsupported feature warnings with spans
- Confidence scores per parse result","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.11","title":"Extract full canvas rendering from FrankenTUI canvas.rs","description":"# Full Canvas Rendering Extraction

Complete extraction of pixel-level canvas rendering from FrankenTUI's canvas.rs (1,651 lines) for fm-render-canvas.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs
- Lines: 1,651
- Purpose: Pixel-perfect canvas rendering for web/WASM

## Current State

fm-render-canvas has basic rendering (~2,200 lines) but needs:
- Full shape rasterization
- Anti-aliased edge drawing
- Text rendering with font metrics
- Layer compositing
- Viewport management

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Shape Rasterization
- Arc and circle drawing (Bresenham)
- Ellipse drawing
- Polygon filling
- Rounded rectangle corners
- All node shape variants

### 2. Edge Rendering
- Line drawing with thickness
- Anti-aliased lines
- Bezier curve approximation
- Arrow head rendering
- Dashed/dotted patterns

### 3. Text Rendering
- Glyph positioning
- Font metrics integration
- Text baseline alignment
- Word wrapping on canvas
- Emoji/Unicode support

### 4. Compositing
- Layer management
- Alpha blending
- Clipping regions
- Dirty rectangle tracking

### 5. Viewport System
- Pan and zoom
- Fit-to-content
- Scroll handling
- High-DPI support

### 6. Performance Features
- Dirty region optimization
- Offscreen buffer caching
- Progressive rendering
- Web Worker offloading

## Canvas API Integration

The reference uses:
- 2D canvas context methods
- Path2D for complex shapes
- ImageData for pixel access
- requestAnimationFrame for animation

fm-render-canvas needs:
- Canvas2dContext trait abstraction
- MockCanvas for testing
- web-sys integration (behind feature flag)

## WASM Considerations

- Memory management for large diagrams
- SharedArrayBuffer for workers
- WebGPU fallback path
- Mobile touch handling

## Acceptance Criteria

- All shapes from reference rendered
- Anti-aliased edges working
- Text rendering accurate
- Viewport interaction working
- Performance: 60fps for 200-node diagrams
- WASM demo rendering correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:11.678931909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:25:14.230757427Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:25:14.230075716Z","close_reason":"Canvas rendering extracted into fm-render-canvas with context.rs (15KB, Canvas2dContext trait + MockCanvas2dContext), renderer.rs (31KB, Canvas2dRenderer), shapes.rs (14KB, shape drawing primitives), viewport.rs (7.5KB, viewport/transform management). Full trait-based abstraction for WASM/web Canvas2D rendering.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","extraction","render","wasm"],"comments":[{"id":73,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"},{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.11","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Canvas primitive drawing (rect, circle, path, text)
2. Coordinate transformation correctness
3. Color handling (RGB, HSL, named colors)
4. Font metrics approximation accuracy

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram render to mock canvas
2. Pixel-accurate comparison with golden bitmaps
3. HiDPI/scaling correctness
4. Clip region handling

### Cross-Platform Tests
1. Native canvas (if implemented)
2. WASM canvas via headless browser
3. OffscreenCanvas support

### Visual Regression
Generate PNG output and compare with golden images:
- Threshold: <1% pixel difference
- Per-diagram-type golden files

### Logging Requirements
- Draw call trace for debugging
- Render timing breakdown
- Canvas state stack trace","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.12","title":"Implement adversarial input handling and security hardening","description":"# Adversarial Input Handling and Security Hardening (Parity and Shared Hardening)

Goal:
- Enforce robust security and bounded runtime behavior for untrusted inputs while preserving parity expectations.

Scope:
- Integrate shared security corpus and SVG sanitization policy.
- Validate parser, layout, and render behavior against adversarial and malformed inputs.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for limits, sanitizer decisions, and threat classification.
- Integration tests for parser to layout to render hardening paths.
- E2E security sweeps with deterministic verdicts and reproducible artifacts.
- Structured logs must include threat class, trigger, sanitizer action, budget usage, fallback path, and pass fail reason.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:31.976024788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:37:53.766892716Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:37:53.766546470Z","close_reason":"All blockers resolved. SVG sanitization policy (bd-1y5.8), adversarial test corpus (bd-2hq), parser completion (bd-2nw.10, bd-106) all closed. Security hardening implemented: URL allowlisting, XML/CSS/SVG-paint sanitization, NaN guards, 12 adversarial tests, 1651+ fuzz corpus entries.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hardening","parser","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.790885694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.427788918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2hq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.306699631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.701233747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":105,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.12","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Adversarial Input Corpus
1. Extremely long node IDs (>10K chars)
2. Deeply nested structures (>100 levels)
3. Massive edge counts (>100K edges)
4. Malformed Unicode sequences
5. Control characters in labels
6. Escape sequence injection attempts

### Security Tests
1. No stack overflow on recursive input
2. Memory bounded for any input size
3. No DoS via pathological input
4. XSS prevention in SVG output
5. No command injection in CLI

### Fuzz Testing
Use cargo-fuzz with sanitizers:
```bash
cargo +nightly fuzz run fuzz_parser -- -max_len=100000
```

### Hardening Validation
1. All panics converted to Result errors
2. Resource limits enforced at all entry points
3. Graceful degradation under attack

### Logging Requirements
- Security-relevant events logged
- Rate of rejected inputs tracked
- Suspicious pattern detection","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:26Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2nw.13","title":"Complete DOT format parser with full Graphviz compatibility","description":"# Complete DOT Format Parser

Extend fm-parser's DOT parser to achieve high compatibility with Graphviz DOT format, enabling import of existing DOT diagrams.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/dot_parser.rs
- Lines: 1,592
- Purpose: Parse Graphviz DOT format into shared IR

## Current State

fm-parser has basic DOT support (~500 lines) but needs:
- Full attribute parsing
- Subgraph/cluster support
- Edge properties
- Record shapes
- HTML labels

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Graph Structure
- digraph (directed)
- graph (undirected)
- strict graphs (no multi-edges)
- subgraph and cluster

### 2. Node Attributes
- shape (box, circle, diamond, record, etc.)
- label (plain and HTML)
- style (solid, dashed, filled)
- color, fillcolor
- fontname, fontsize
- width, height
- pos (for importing positioned graphs)

### 3. Edge Attributes
- label
- style (solid, dashed, dotted)
- color
- arrowhead, arrowtail
- headlabel, taillabel
- constraint

### 4. Graph Attributes
- rankdir (TB, LR, BT, RL)
- bgcolor
- splines
- nodesep, ranksep
- compound

### 5. Special Features
- Record shapes (nested fields)
- HTML-like labels
- Edge ports (n, ne, e, se, s, sw, w, nw)
- ID quoting rules
- Comments (// and /* */)
- Preprocessor directives

### 6. Compatibility Levels

| Level | Features |
|-------|----------|
| Basic | digraph, nodes, edges |
| Standard | Attributes, clusters |
| Extended | Records, HTML labels |
| Full | Ports, preprocessor |

## Mapping to IR

```
DOT node → IrNode
DOT edge → IrEdge  
DOT subgraph → IrCluster
DOT attribute → IrNode.classes or style hints
```

## Testing

- Graphviz test suite compatibility
- Round-trip: DOT → IR → DOT
- Large real-world DOT files
- Edge cases from Graphviz docs

## Acceptance Criteria

- All common DOT features parsed
- Attribute mapping to IR complete
- Record shapes working
- HTML labels handled
- Test coverage: Graphviz examples
- Documentation of compatibility level","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:18:49.361263986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.421290930Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dot","graphviz","parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.13","depends_on_id":"bd-106","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:59.879327938Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":91,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:02Z"},{"id":106,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.13","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### DOT Grammar Coverage
1. All node shapes (box, circle, diamond, etc.)
2. All edge styles (solid, dashed, dotted)
3. Subgraph/cluster syntax
4. Attribute lists on nodes/edges/graphs
5. Port connections
6. Record-based labels

### Graphviz Compatibility Tests
Use Graphviz test corpus:
1. Parse all Graphviz examples
2. Compare layout with reference output
3. Track compatibility percentage

### Integration Tests
1. DOT to IR conversion correctness
2. IR to layout to render pipeline
3. Round-trip: DOT -> IR -> DOT (where applicable)

### Logging Requirements
- Parse trace with DOT-specific tokens
- Attribute value extraction details
- Unsupported attribute warnings","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:34Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2nw.14","title":"Implement advanced edge routing with orthogonal and spline paths","description":"# Advanced Edge Routing

Implement sophisticated edge routing algorithms from FrankenTUI to support orthogonal routing, curved edges, and proper edge-node avoidance.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs
- Lines: ~800 (routing section)
- Purpose: High-quality edge paths

## Current State

fm-layout has basic edge routing but lacks:
- Orthogonal (right-angle) routing
- Node avoidance
- Edge-edge avoidance
- Port assignment
- Curved edge smoothing

## Key Features to Implement

### 1. Orthogonal Router

**Algorithm**: Visibility graph + A* pathfinding

Steps:
1. Build obstacle grid (nodes as rectangles)
2. Compute visibility graph
3. Find shortest orthogonal path
4. Apply bend minimization
5. Assign edge channels

**Properties:**
- O(n²) visibility computation
- O(e log n) pathfinding per edge
- Guaranteed to find path if exists

### 2. Edge Channel Assignment

When multiple edges share a path segment:
1. Compute channel requirements
2. Assign channels to minimize crossings
3. Apply channel-specific offsets
4. Preserve edge ordering

### 3. Port Assignment

For nodes with multiple edges:
1. Group edges by direction
2. Assign port positions
3. Minimize edge crossings at ports
4. Handle multi-edge bundles

### 4. Curved Edge Smoothing

Convert orthogonal paths to smooth curves:
1. Identify corner points
2. Compute Bezier control points
3. Generate cubic Bezier segments
4. Preserve tangent continuity

### 5. Node Avoidance

Ensure edges don't cross through nodes:
1. Detect potential crossings
2. Route around obstacles
3. Minimize detour length
4. Handle nested clusters

### 6. Long Edge Handling

For edges spanning multiple layers:
1. Create dummy nodes at each layer
2. Route through dummies
3. Align dummy nodes for straightness
4. Handle wide nodes

## Rendering Integration

Edge paths output as:
```rust
pub enum EdgePath {
    Straight { from: Point, to: Point },
    Orthogonal { segments: Vec<(Point, Point)> },
    Bezier { segments: Vec<BezierCubic> },
}
```

## Performance Targets

| Graph Size | Routing Time |
|------------|--------------|
| 100 nodes | <50ms |
| 500 nodes | <200ms |
| 1000 nodes | <500ms |

## Acceptance Criteria

- Orthogonal routing working
- No edge-node intersections
- Port assignment minimizing crossings
- Curved edges option available
- Visual parity with FrankenTUI
- Performance within targets","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:07.733066831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T18:05:27.757112707Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T18:05:27.756626616Z","close_reason":"Implemented node-aware orthogonal edge routing: edges now detect when midpoint segments intersect intermediate node bounding boxes and reroute around them. Added route_edge_points_with_obstacles(), find_obstacle_nudge_x/y() for axis-aligned avoidance. 3 new tests. Full A* pathfinding deferred to follow-up.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["edges","layout","routing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:51.788304429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":72,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:27:59Z"},{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.14","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Orthogonal Routing Tests
1. Channel assignment correctness
2. No edge crossings through nodes
3. Minimum bend count
4. Path length optimization

### Spline Path Tests
1. Bezier control point calculation
2. Smooth curve through waypoints
3. No self-intersecting curves
4. Consistent curvature

### Integration Tests
1. Full diagram with mixed edge styles
2. Layout determinism with edge routing
3. Performance with many edges (>1000)

### Visual Regression
Golden output for edge routing scenarios:
- Dense graphs with many crossings
- Sparse graphs with long paths
- Hierarchical layouts

### Logging Requirements
- Routing algorithm trace
- Channel occupancy map
- Bend points selected","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.15","title":"Build automated feature parity verification against FrankenTUI","description":"# Automated Feature Parity Verification against FrankenTUI

Goal:
- Generate objective parity evidence that combines capability metadata, E2E telemetry, and proof artifacts into release consumable reports.

Scope:
- Build feature matrix generation from shared capability sources and runtime evidence.
- Compare reference and FM outputs semantically and perceptually with explicit confidence tags.
- Publish machine readable and human readable parity dashboards.

Verification and logging requirements:
- Unit tests for matrix generation, scoring rules, and threshold evaluation.
- Integration tests for ingestion of reference capture plus CLI and WASM artifacts.
- E2E parity runs with deterministic replay and triage outputs.
- Structured logs must include feature id, support state, reference verdict, FM verdict, delta type, confidence, artifact links, and pass fail reason.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:26.913410616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:30:32.757022782Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.776755747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.120837335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.208363086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.866478867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:41.952552130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:42.036757681Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.913294434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.550949443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.24","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.793558913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","depends_on_id":"bd-3uz.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:25:52.674886281Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":108,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.15","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Feature Parity Matrix
Automated verification against FrankenTUI:
1. All diagram types parsed
2. All node shapes rendered
3. All edge styles supported
4. All styling options work

### Automated Comparison
```bash
# scripts/parity-check.sh
for sample in corpus/*.mmd; do
  frankentui_output=$(frankentui render $sample)
  frankenmermaid_output=$(fm-cli render $sample)
  compare_outputs $frankentui_output $frankenmermaid_output
done
```

### Coverage Tracking
1. Feature checklist with pass/fail status
2. Percentage coverage metric
3. Regression detection on coverage drop

### Logging Requirements
- Detailed comparison trace
- Difference categorization (visual, structural, semantic)
- Prioritized gap list","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:29:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nw.2","title":"Extract minimap rendering from mermaid_minimap.rs into fm-render-term","description":"# Minimap Rendering Extraction

Extract the complete minimap functionality from FrankenTUI's mermaid_minimap.rs (1,474 lines) into frankenmermaid's fm-render-term crate.

## Reference Code
- Source: /dp/frankentui/crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_minimap.rs
- Lines: 1,474
- Purpose: Overview visualization of large diagrams with viewport indicator

## Key Features to Extract

### 1. Minimap Computation
- Proportional scaling to fit overview area
- Node density-based detail level selection
- Viewport rectangle calculation
- Pan/zoom coordinate mapping

### 2. Rendering Modes
- Terminal minimap (block characters)
- Simple minimap (ASCII only)
- Colored minimap (highlight regions)

### 3. Viewport Interaction
- Viewport position calculation
- Click-to-navigate coordinate mapping
- Viewport size indicator

### 4. Performance Features
- Lazy rendering (only visible regions)
- Cached minimap with dirty tracking
- Progressive detail rendering

## Implementation Notes

The FrankenTUI minimap uses:
- Downsampling algorithms for node representation
- Edge simplification (straight lines only)
- Color quantization for terminal output

Key adaptations needed:
1. Remove ftui-style dependencies
2. Integrate with fm-layout positioned nodes
3. Support multiple output targets (term, SVG, canvas)
4. Add streaming/progressive rendering

## Acceptance Criteria

- All minimap modes from reference implemented
- Unit tests for scaling/viewport math
- Integration with fm-render-term
- Visual regression tests
- Performance: <50ms for 1000-node diagrams","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:27.959621218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:24:48.828085908Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:24:48.827224627Z","close_reason":"Minimap rendering extracted into fm-render-term/src/minimap.rs (21KB). Includes MinimapConfig, MinimapResult, scaled overview rendering with viewport indicator and proportional positioning.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extraction","minimap","render"],"comments":[{"id":78,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification contract (mandatory): this bead must ship comprehensive unit tests, integration coverage, and E2E script coverage with detailed structured logging. Required logs include input metadata, algorithm or mode selection, diagnostics, degradation or fallback decisions where applicable, timing metrics, output hashes or sizes, and explicit pass fail reasons. CI artifacts must be sufficient for post-mortem debugging without rerun.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:00Z"},{"id":97,"issue_id":"bd-2nw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
1. Proportional scaling calculation correctness
2. Viewport rectangle computation for various zoom levels
3. Click coordinate mapping to diagram position
4. Detail level selection based on node density

### Integration Tests
1. Minimap renders correctly for diagrams of various sizes
2. Viewport indicator tracks main view accurately
3. Pan/zoom coordinate mapping is bijective

### Visual Regression Tests
Use golden output comparisons for:
- Small diagram minimap (< 10 nodes)
- Medium diagram (10-50 nodes)
- Large diagram (100+ nodes with clusters)

### Logging Requirements
- Structured trace of viewport calculations
- Render timing per detail level
- Memory usage for cached minimap","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:28:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2u0.5.3.3","title":"Implement persistent URL/state model for reproducible deep links","description":"## Objective
Make demo states reproducible and shareable.

## Subtasks
- Encode scenario, theme, renderer, and toggles in URL-safe state.
- Implement load/save semantics with validation/fallback.
- Provide copy-link affordance for exact state sharing.

## Evidence
Shared links reproduce equivalent state deterministically.

## Verification, Test, and Logging Requirements (Mandatory)
- Unit tests: add or update focused unit tests for every function/module touched by this bead, including edge cases and error paths.
- Integration tests: add or update integration coverage for cross-module behavior and any contract boundaries changed by this bead.
- End-to-end scripts: add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise this bead's user-visible behavior on relevant surfaces (`/web`, `/web_react`, CLI/WASM where applicable).
- Structured logging: emit and validate machine-readable logs for test runs with at least: `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `surface`, `renderer`, `theme`, `config_hash`, `parse_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `degradation_tier`, `output_artifact_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Determinism checks: run repeat executions for representative scenarios and assert stable outputs/log summaries when determinism is expected.
- Evidence artifacts: store test/log artifacts so they are reviewable and reproducible; failures must include actionable context and suggested remediation.
- Planning/research-only beads: even when no runtime code changes, add validation scripts/checklists that verify artifact completeness, schema consistency, and decision-trace logging.","notes":"Implemented persistent URL/state reproduction in frankenmermaid_demo_showcase.html.

Key outputs:
- added URL-safe state serialization for sample, spotlight, gallery filter/query, source override, and shell balances
- added restore-on-load behavior with validation/fallback to canonical scenario IDs
- added popstate handling so browser navigation restores equivalent showcase state
- added Copy Link affordance for exact state sharing from the playground controls
- kept the state model aligned with existing shell/scenario state instead of introducing a separate store

Validation:
- local html.parser feed passed
- state helper wiring checked against existing sample/gallery/shell transitions

Primary downstream effect: editor and diagnostics beads can now assume reproducible deep-link state exists.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:24.261023688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:26:16.982200580Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:26:16.981997205Z","close_reason":"Implemented URL-safe reproducible showcase state and share-link flow","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","state","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.5.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:24.261023688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.5.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:27.673408596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2u0.5.4","title":"Implement live authoring loop with diagnostics-first feedback","description":"## Why this exists
Live authoring is the core interactive loop. It must feel immediate, informative, and safe under malformed input.

## Scope
- Rich editor integration.
- Debounced/cancellable pipeline from text to render.
- Diagnostics and fallback UX.

## Deliverables
- Authoring loop with low-latency feedback.
- Actionable diagnostics integrated with source text.
- Stable fallback rendering path for failure modes.

## Acceptance Evidence
Typing/editing, diagnostics, and rendering remain responsive and informative under both normal and malformed inputs.

## Verification, Test, and Logging Requirements (Mandatory)
- Unit tests: add or update focused unit tests for every function/module touched by this bead, including edge cases and error paths.
- Integration tests: add or update integration coverage for cross-module behavior and any contract boundaries changed by this bead.
- End-to-end scripts: add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise this bead's user-visible behavior on relevant surfaces (`/web`, `/web_react`, CLI/WASM where applicable).
- Structured logging: emit and validate machine-readable logs for test runs with at least: `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `surface`, `renderer`, `theme`, `config_hash`, `parse_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `degradation_tier`, `output_artifact_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Determinism checks: run repeat executions for representative scenarios and assert stable outputs/log summaries when determinism is expected.
- Evidence artifacts: store test/log artifacts so they are reviewable and reproducible; failures must include actionable context and suggested remediation.
- Planning/research-only beads: even when no runtime code changes, add validation scripts/checklists that verify artifact completeness, schema consistency, and decision-trace logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:24.357694813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T05:54:19.771620249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","diagnostics","editor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:24.357694813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:26.428387178Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:26.532003432Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":114,"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency rationale: this track exists as an execution lane with explicit upstream blockers and concrete downstream evidence obligations so contributors can act without consulting external planning docs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2u0.5.4.1","title":"Integrate rich editor surface with syntax and productivity affordances","description":"## Objective
Integrate an editor surface suitable for Mermaid-like authoring.

## Subtasks
- Add syntax highlighting and structural hints.
- Add keyboard shortcuts for scenario switching and run/refresh.

## Verification, Test, and Logging Requirements (Mandatory)

 {"id":"bd-2u0.5.4.3","title":"Implement inline diagnostics, remediation hints, and safe fallback preview","description":"## Objective
Ensure users understand and recover from issues quickly.

 {"id":"bd-2u0.5.5","title":"Implement feature spotlight modules for core differentiators","description":"## Why this exists
The demo must explicitly spotlight differentiators instead of hoping users discover them. This track packages capabilities into guided, testable modules.

## Scope
- Layout/cycle strategy explorer.
- Theme/style studio.
- Export artifact studio.
- Feature-complete gallery/compare mode.

## Deliverables
- Distinct showcase modules with clear educational framing.
- Module-level controls and validation artifacts.
- Unified module navigation and state interoperability.

## Acceptance Evidence
A reviewer can verify each major capability by running named showcase modules end-to-end.

## Verification, Test, and Logging Requirements (Mandatory)
- Unit tests: add or update focused unit tests for every function/module touched by this bead, including edge cases and error paths.
- Integration tests: add or update integration coverage for cross-module behavior and any contract boundaries changed by this bead.
- End-to-end scripts: add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise this bead's user-visible behavior on relevant surfaces (`/web`, `/web_react`, CLI/WASM where applicable).
- Structured logging: emit and validate machine-readable logs for test runs with at least: `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `surface`, `renderer`, `theme`, `config_hash`, `parse_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `degradation_tier`, `output_artifact_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Determinism checks: run repeat executions for representative scenarios and assert stable outputs/log summaries when determinism is expected.
- Evidence artifacts: store test/log artifacts so they are reviewable and reproducible; failures must include actionable context and suggested remediation.
- Planning/research-only beads: even when no runtime code changes, add validation scripts/checklists that verify artifact completeness, schema consistency, and decision-trace logging.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:24.755713848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T05:54:19.138166493Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","features","showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:24.755713848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2u0.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:26.632971710Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":115,"issue_id":"bd-2u0.5.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Dependency rationale: this track exists as an execution lane with explicit upstream blockers and concrete downstream evidence obligations so contributors can act without consulting external planning docs.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:30Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2u0.5.5.1","title":"Build layout and cycle-strategy lab with comparative visual controls","description":"## Objective
Show layout intelligence visibly.

## Subtasks
- Expose algorithm and cycle strategy controls.
- Visualize effects on crossings, edge routing, and graph legibility.
- Surface relevant layout stats for each run.

## Evidence
Users can compare layout strategies on the same input and inspect outcome metrics.

## Verification, Test, and Logging Requirements (Mandatory)
- Unit tests: add or update focused unit tests for every function/module touched by this bead, including edge cases and error paths.
- Integration tests: add or update integration coverage for cross-module behavior and any contract boundaries changed by this bead.
- End-to-end scripts: add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise this bead's user-visible behavior on relevant surfaces (`/web`, `/web_react`, CLI/WASM where applicable).

- Determinism checks: run repeat executions for representative scenarios and assert stable outputs/log summaries when determinism is expected.
- Evidence artifacts: store test/log artifacts so they are reviewable and reproducible; failures must include actionable context and suggested remediation.
